title: Conservation professionals
description: Manage, improve, and protect natural resources to maximize their use without damaging the environment. May conduct soil surveys and develop plans to eliminate soil erosion or to protect rangelands. May instruct farmers, agricultural production managers, or ranchers in best ways to use crop rotation, contour plowing, or terracing to conserve soil and water; in the number and kind of livestock and forage plants best suited to particular ranges; and in range and farm improvements, such as fencing and reservoirs for stock watering. Research or study range land management practices to provide sustained production of forage, livestock, and wildlife. Plan, develop, and conduct programs to inform public of historical, natural, and scientific features of national, state, or local park. (Based on 7 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Conduct environmental audits, inspections, or investigations of violations and evaluate problems discovered to determine appropriate regulatory actions or to provide advice on the development and prosecution of regulatory cases.

Redesign linear, or open-loop, systems into cyclical, or closed-loop, systems so that waste products become inputs for new processes, modeling natural ecosystems and analyze changes designed to improve the environmental performance of complex systems.

Conduct applied research on the effects of industrial processes on the protection, restoration, inventory, monitoring, or reintroduction of species to the natural environment and examine local, regional, or global use and flow of materials or energy in industrial production processes.
Recommend methods to protect the environment or minimize environmental damage from industrial production practices and develop sustainable alternatives to industrial or waste-management practices.

Analyze the effectiveness of industrial ecology programs, conduct environmental sustainability assessments, and analyze data to determine the validity, quality, and scientific significance, to interpret correlations between human activities and environmental effects.
Prepare plans for managing renewable resources, including developing natural resource management plans, habitat management, or restoration plans such as reforestation and weed control strategies.
Conduct site assessments to identify environmental damages or restoration needs and monitor the environmental impact of development activities, pollution, or land degradation.

Identify and advise on strategies or methods to minimize the environmental impact of industrial production processes and promote the use of environmental management systems to reduce waste or improve the environmentally sound use of natural resources.

Conduct field research on ecological implications of changes in industrial processes, monitor environmental impacts of development activities, and conduct environmental impact studies to examine ecological effects of pollutants and human activities.

Plan, develop, and implement educational programs on environmental issues for public understanding and awareness, and communicate findings of environmental studies to stakeholders, including parks personnel and government agencies.
Coordinate with federal land managers to manage and protect rangelands and advise land users on conservation planning including water management and forage production methods.
Develop or maintain working relationships with local government staff, manage field offices, and supervise or train staff and students in environmental and conservation practices.

Gather information from geographic information systems databases or applications to formulate land-use recommendations and develop or test protocols to monitor ecosystem components and ecological processes.
Conduct field trips to showcase scientific, historic, and natural features of parks, and provide visitor services by answering requests and explaining regulations.

Prepare technical reports, including environmental impact reports, and write grant applications or proposals for funding restoration projects while managing budgets and project schedules for environmental restoration efforts.
Plan, implement, and coordinate environmental restoration strategies, focusing on compliance with regulations and technical standards, and identifying environmental mitigation alternatives.
Monitor projects during construction or after to ensure compliance with design specifications and review existing environmental remediation designs for adherence to standards.
Collect, synthesize, analyze, and report environmental data, such as monitoring measurements or soil and water samples, to determine environmental conditions and restoration needs.
Study rangeland management practices and analyze grazing patterns to determine sustainable livestock practices, while advising on conservation solutions tailored to landowners' goals.

Communicate scientific or technical information to the public, organizations, or internal audiences through various means such as oral briefings, written documents, workshops, and public hearings. Provide scientific or technical guidance and support to governmental agencies, environmental programs, industry, or the public. Process and review environmental permits, licenses, or related materials. Conduct applied research on environmental topics, including waste control or pollution abatement methods. Review existing policies or legislation to identify environmental impacts and write reports or academic papers to communicate findings of climate-related studies. Promote initiatives to mitigate climate change with government or environmental groups and present climate-related information at public meetings. Prepare grant applications for programs related to climate change, environmental management, or sustainability. Gather and review climate-related studies from various organizations. Develop or contribute to educational or outreach programs on the environment or climate change.
